title: Apollo asteroid
content:
The Apollo asteroids are a group of near-Earth asteroid s named after 1862 Apollo, discovered by German astronomer Karl Reinmuth in the 1930s. They are Earth-crossing asteroid s that have an orbital semi-major axis greater than that of the Earth (1 <a href="Astronomical%20unit" AU</a) but <a href="perihelion" perihelion</a distances less than the Earth's <a href="aphelion" aphelion</a distance (q < 1.017 AU).

the number of known Apollo asteroids is 10,485, making the class the largest group of <a href="near-Earth%20object" near-Earth object</a s ("cf".
